Chris Brazzell to the Panthers:

6’4” with 4.37 speed

1,017 yards and 9 TDs in 2025

2.30 yards per team pass attempt (WR7)

48.8% of targets = first down or TD (WR6)

Brazzell ran 93.2% of his snaps out wide (highest in class)

15.7 air yards per target (WR4)

Deep, explosive, vertical usage

3.1 YPRR vs zone (WR4)

But 2.05 YPRR vs man (WR25)

but he comes from the Josh Heupel system — where WRs (Hyatt, Tillman, Thornton) feast on wide splits, space, and one-on-one matchups… then struggle to translate

Then the physical concerns:

198 lbs with 9” hands

Adjusted catch radius was 4th lowest in class

41% catch rate on contested throws (WR35 in class)

New ESPN story: Since Jayson Tatum’s rookie year, no one has played more NBA playoff games than either he or Jaylen Brown.

That experience in the playoff pressure cooker was on display down the stretch in South Philly Friday in powering Boston to a win.

https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/48586481/jayson-tatum-jaylen-brown-come-big-celtics-fun-game-3-win

Final: Celtics 108, Sixers 100

Thanks to the dynamic duo of Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown, the Celtics survive a flurry from Tyrese Maxey to reclaim home court advantage in this series.

Game 4 is back here in South Philly in 48 hours. Will Joel Embiid take part?
Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown have carried the Celtics home tonight with some absolutely big time shot making.
Just some absolute haymaker shots at both ends. Wow.

And now the Sixers have come back with five straight points of their own and Boston calls timeout.

Some awesome end-to-end stuff here down the stretch from both teams.
Tremendous response here from the Celtics, who have scored eight unanswered points and silenced the crowd here in Philly while giving Boston a 92-85 lead with 5:30 to go in the game.

Tyrese Maxey is now 8-for-11 and has 20 points in the second half, and his last 3-pointer has Philly up 85-84 with 8:41 to go.

This is going to be an awfully fun finish here.
Tyrese Maxey is in the game to start the fourth quarter. Guessing he will be going the rest of the way as Philly tries to steal Game 3 here.

After 3: Celtics 79, Sixers 74

Boston is 16-for-39 from 3 tonight, and Philly is hanging around thanks to an 18-8 edge in points off turnovers.

Jaylen Brown has 17 for Boston and Tyrese Maxey has 21 (on 21 shots) for Philly.

Should be a wild final 12 minutes.
Tyrese Maxey is The Human Torch.
The Celtics have already made more 3s tonight (14-for-32) than they did in all of Game 2 (13-for-50) and we are two minutes into the third quarter.

After going 13-for-50 from 3-point range in Game 2, the Celtics went 7-for-16 in the first quarter tonight alone.

One thing that isn't going to change, no matter the shooting results, is Boston's approach.

The Celtics under Joe Mazzulla are going to let it fly.
